title: 6 AFT Trace Requests
body_text:
6	AFT Trace Requests
6.1	Overview of AFT Trace Requests
Initiate an AFT trace request if an outgoing AFT transaction was expected by the recipient at another financial institution but was not received on the Due Date. The Processing Direct Clearer is not obligated to trace AFT transactions for $20 or less or to process trace requests received more than 12 months after the transaction Settlement Date.
Financial institutions can also initiate an AFT trace request to identify an AFT transaction that was posted to a memberГўВҖВҷs account if the identity of the sender is unknown.
6.2	Initiating AFT Trace Requests in ServiceNow
Use ServiceNow to initiate a trace request for incoming or outgoing AFT transactions.
Review the Central 1 reports as described below prior to creating a trace request for an
incoming AFT transaction
(i.e., a transaction that was posted to your memberГўВҖВҷs account).
Incoming Transactions to Financial Institutions
Obtain the Due Date of the transaction from the member. If the transaction is due today, but has not been posted, look for it in todayГўВҖВҷs AFT clearing reports as follows:
Canadian AFT transactions ГўВҖВ“ Check the CCD report in the CRF* files that are available after each Run. For BC, it will be in the CR0* Runs and for Ontario it will be in the ON0* Runs.
USD AFT transactions ГўВҖВ“ Check the UCD report from MERG file. For BC, it will be the UC01 Run and for Ontario it will be the UN01 Run.
If a credit transaction is reported, but not posted to the memberГўВҖВҷs account, you must release funds to the member. See AFT Settlement Part, Section 5.4, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts ГўВҖВ” Manually.
Follow the steps below if the Due Date is not today:
Look for the transaction on the CCD/UCD report in the MERG file of the same date as the Due Date. Look in the CCD for the CR0* Runs in BC or the ON0* Runs in Ontario. Each Run has the following line in the header: ГўВҖВңCRXX for mm/dd/yyГўВҖВқ or ГўВҖВңONXX for mm/dd/yyГўВҖВқ, as applicable.
If the transaction is not in that dayГўВҖВҷs MERG, look in the MERG file for the following day, and so on. Remember to look at all Runs in the CCD for Canadian dollar AFT transactions. In BC, Runs appear in the order CR03, CR04, CR01, and CR02. In Ontario, Runs appear in the order ON03, ON04, ON01, and ON02. Do not look in the MERG file for a date earlier than the Due Date because the transaction will not be reported earlier than the Due Date.
Check the UC01 in BC or the UN01 in Ontario on the UCD report for US dollar AFT transactions.
Note the Run number and the ГўВҖВңforГўВҖВқ date displayed in the report header once the transaction is on the CCD report for Canadian dollar transactions or on the UCD report for US dollar transactions. Determine the day on which your financial institution or banking system provider would have posted the item, depending on your posting schedule (i.e., some banking systems post each Run as it becomes available, but others only post once a day or only on business days). See AFT Settlement Part, Section 5.3, Posting Incoming AFT Transactions to Member Accounts ГўВҖВ” Automatically via Banking System, to determine when Run CR01-04 and UC01 are made available.
Contact Central 1 if the transaction cannot be located on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD/UCD)
Initiating a Trace Request in ServiceNow
Proceed as follows to initiate a trace request in ServiceNow for outgoing and incoming AFT transactions:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Note: 	YouГўВҖВҷll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if itГўВҖВҷs your first login to the Client Centre, youГўВҖВҷve deleted your cookies, or youГўВҖВҷre using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 1: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
, then select
Production Applications
. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
ServiceNow
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youГўВҖВҷve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under ГўВҖВңMy FavouritesГўВҖВқ.
Figure 3: Accessing ServiceNow
Select
Service Catalogue
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: ServiceNow ГўВҖВ“ Accessing the Service Catalogue
Select the
Electronic Payments
category
and then select
AFT Trace Request
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Selecting the AFT Trace Request Form
Complete the AFT Trace Request page as follows. See Figure 6 and Figure 7.
Note:	Mandatory fields are highlighted red.
ГўВ‘
Enter your Transit Number.
ГўВ‘ВЎ
Use the drop-down menu to select one of the following request types.
ГўВҲВ’
Single Incoming AFT Transaction
ГўВҲВ’
Multiple Incoming AFT Transactions
ГўВҲВ’
Single Outgoing AFT Transaction
ГўВҲВ’
Multiple Outgoing AFT Transactions
ГўВ‘Вў
Enter the OriginatorГўВҖВҷs information. This section is only displayed when tracing single or multiple outgoing AFT transactions.
ГўВ‘ВЈ
If tracing a single incoming or outgoing AFT transaction, enter the details of the transaction. See Figure 6.
Alternatively, if tracing multiple incoming or outgoing AFT transactions, select the
Click here to download
link to complete the spreadsheet. The fields in the spreadsheet are generally the same as those displayed in the Transaction Details section for single transactions. Save the file and then select the
Click here to attach files
link to upload the file to ServiceNow. Follow the prompts. See Figure 7.
ГўВ‘ВӨ
Enter additional comments if necessary.
ГўВ‘ВҘ
Select
Submit Form
. The request is sent to Central 1 and a ServiceNow ticket number is created.
Figure 6: Trace Request ГўВҖВ“ Single Transaction
Figure 7: Trace Request ГўВҖВ“ Multiple Transactions
Wait for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs response. Central 1 will contact the Originator shortly after the trace response is received. CPA Rule F1,
Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions,
stipulates that Central 1 must receive a response to the trace:
ГўВҲВ’
within two business days of the date of receipt of the request, for trace requests with Due Dates of up to 30 days, or
ГўВҲВ’
within five business days of the date of the receipt of the request, for traces requests with Due Dates of greater than 30 days.
Exception:	If credit transactions are received with insufficient Lead Time, the Processing Direct Clearer is not required to act on the trace for a minimum of two business days from receipt of the AFT file.
6.3	Initiating AFT Trace Requests Manually
If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete the AFT Trace Request, Form 3296, to initiate a trace request for incoming or outgoing AFT transactions. Central 1 will only accept this form if ServiceNow is unavailable.
Prior to initiating trace requests for incoming AFT transactions, review the Central 1 reports as described in Section 6.2, Initiating AFT Trace Requests via ServiceNow.
Proceed as follows:
Complete the AFT Trace Request, Form 3296. Completion of the form is self-explanatory. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: AFT Trace Request, Form 3296
Have the form signed by one signing officer on file with Central 1.
Send the form to Central 1 as directed on the form.
Wait for Central 1ГўВҖВҷs response. Central 1 will contact the Originator shortly after the trace response is received. CPA Rule F1, Rules Applicable to Automated Funds Transfer (AFT) Transactions, stipulates that Central 1 must receive a response to the trace:
ГўВҲВ’
within two business days of the date of receipt of the request, for trace requests with Due Dates of up to 30 days, or
ГўВҲВ’
within five business days of the date of the receipt of the request, for traces requests with Due Dates of greater than 30 days.
Exception:	If credit transactions are received with insufficient Lead Time, the Processing Direct Clearer is not required to act on the trace for a minimum of two business days from receipt of the AFT file.
Related Information
AFT Trace Request,
Form 3296
6.4	Receiving an AFT Trace Request
Receiving an Inquiry
If a trace request is received from an Originating Direct Clearer, Central 1 contacts the Receiving Financial Institution to confirm receipt of the AFT transaction.
The financial institution proceeds as follows:
Record the details of the transaction as provided by Central 1 (account number, Transit Number, account name, Due Date, and amount).
Check the memberГўВҖВҷs account and verify that the transaction was posted. If it was manually posted to the account, check the account name and account number closely.
Advise Central 1 of the posting date as soon as possible. Financial institutions must adhere to CPA tracing timelines.
Receiving a Trace and Return Request
The Originator from another financial institution can submit a trace and return request if the transaction has been posted to the memberГўВҖВҷs account, but an automatic Error Correction is no longer possible. Central 1 will contact the financial institution with a trace and return request via telephone. The other financial institution is aware that these are processed on a best efforts basis.
Financial institutions are not obligated to process these requests. If you decide to proceed, ensure that you obtain authorization from the member.
The financial institution proceeds as follows if a trace and return request is received from Central 1:
Contact the member and advise that the Originator has requested an Error Correction.
Obtain approval from the member to reverse the original credit or debit transaction.
Return the transaction to the Originator. Enter the return on the Online Return System (ORS) or on alternate software and use code 903 as the return reason code. If returned, the transaction is posted on the Batch Returns Error List Report (ICBR/UCBR).
END
